design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally scrolling stages while collecting power-ups, managing two distinct ship forms (jet vs. mech), and deploying limited special ability charges to overcome enemy waves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward of form switching: jet form offers focused forward damage and speed but limited angles; mech form offers defensive flexibility and wider coverage but slower horizontal movement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a transformable prototype mecha-fighter, inheriting your grandfather's technology to defend humanity against alien invasion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Two distinct mechanical forms with asymmetrical tradeoffs force players to commit to form strategies rather than providing a unified optimal path; power-ups persist between levels, rewarding aggressive collection but penalizing death with total loss

difficulty.lives_system: Single-hit destruction unless energy shield equipped (shields sustain three hits before depletion)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; power-ups retained between stages but lost entirely on death within a stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two Arrow Flash modes provide difficulty scaling: 'Stock' mode limits uses (harder resource management), 'Charge' mode allows infinite use with weakened effect (more forgiving but less powerful)

progression.upgrade_system: Dynamic weapon system: basic shot upgradeable/exchangeable for different weapons; acquisition of helper ships (drones following player ship that copy attacks); energy shields providing temporary invulnerability; power-ups scattered throughout levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Power-ups found in-stage; one downscrolling stage available alongside primary horizontal scrolling stages
replayability_hooks: Form-switching mastery encourages replays with different strategies (jet-focus vs. mech-focus)
Two Arrow Flash charge modes create distinct difficulty/capability curves
Power-up collection optimization for speedrun potential
High review variance suggests skill-based replayability for mastery

world_building_techniques: Character backstory via inheritance plot (protagonist Zana Keene receives prototype fighter from grandfather)
Cultural references to mecha anime franchises (Gundam, Macross) establish tone
Alien invasion premise provides minimal but sufficient context for stage progression

faction_indicators: Humanity vs. alien invaders (established through narrative framing)
Protagonist Zana Keene positioned as defender of humankind
danger_signals: Alien attack on humanity creates existential threat
Progressive stage-based escalation implies rising invasion intensity
environmental_storytelling: Mecha aesthetic conveys high-tech military response
Horizontal scrolling progression through stages suggests territorial defense
Form-switching mechanic (jet/mech) mirrors tactical adaptation to threats
